title: baccharis racemosa var. eupatorioides
intro: Baccharis racemosa var. eupatorioides, also known as False Wormwood or Coyote Mint, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its distinctive minty aroma and medicinal properties. This plant is used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of Baccharis racemosa var. eupatorioides include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. Baccharis racemosa var. eupatorioides can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
